channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: Bridging Design Systems and Code with MCP and AI Agents
item.description: IBM Technology: IBM presents an approach using context engineering and Model Context Protocol (MCP) to align AI agents with design systems, enabling agents to build consistent and correct applications. The video discusses how design system constraints can be integrated into agentic workflows through contextual guidance.

item.title: Build a Hermes Knowledge Base That Self-Improves
item.description: Jack Roberts: The video demonstrates how to augment AI agents like Hermes with self-improving knowledge bases by combining retrieval-augmented generation principles with automated ingestion pipelines. It connects personal knowledge management tools (Obsidian wiki), LLM services (Claude, NotebookLM), and meeting capture (Granola) via cron jobs to create a two-way memory system that persists knowledge across agent interactions.

item.title: RIP Claude Fable, open-source AI unleashed, full body avatars, new Google models, new TTS: AI NEWS
item.description: AI Search: AI Search channel reviews multiple AI developments including Kimi K2.7, MiniMax M3, GLM 5.2, and various research projects on video, avatars, and text-to-speech. The episode covers emerging models and tools across generative AI, computer vision, and speech synthesis domains.

item.title: Is This The FASTEST AI Model In The World?!! (Xiaomi MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed)
item.description: Better Stack: Xiaomi and TileRT released a 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model that achieves over 1,000 tokens per second on standard hardware. The model combines selective FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and TileRT's persistent GPU kernel runtime to optimize inference speed.

item.title: Fable 5 is never coming back, here's why
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej reports that Fable 5 has been banned and frames this as marking the beginning of a permanent underclass. The video title suggests the ban means Fable 5 will not return.

item.title: This Is Bad... They Just Shut Down FABLE 5
item.description: AI Revolution: The video discusses the shutdown of Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models after three days due to a US government order, raising concerns about whether public access to frontier AI systems will be restricted. The author argues this incident signals a broader pattern of government control over powerful AI capabilities.

item.title: GPT-6 Is Launching Into a World OpenAI No Longer Controls
item.description: AI Master: A video essay analyzing GPT-6 rumors and OpenAI's competitive position, claiming that Microsoft, Apple, and Google have moved away from OpenAI by adopting competing solutions like Project Polaris, Gemini, and Claude. The piece discusses benchmark performance, the Goblin incident, memory capabilities, and speculates on whether GPT-6 can restore OpenAI's market dominance.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
item.description: Cole Medin: Cole Medin discusses the US government ordering Anthropic to withdraw Claude Fable 5 from availability due to export-control concerns and a claimed jailbreak vulnerability. The video covers the regulatory action, why the model was pulled overnight, and its performance benchmarks relative to Claude Opus 4.8.

item.title: 1,000+ Tokens/Sec: Google Just Shattered the AI Speed Limit (DiffusionGemma)
item.description: Better Stack: Google DeepMind released DiffusionGemma, a text generation model that applies diffusion techniques from image generation to achieve token generation speeds exceeding 1,000 tokens per second on H100 GPUs. The model uses uniform state diffusion and bidirectional passes to refine text generation, shifting the computational bottleneck from memory-bound to compute-bound operations.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
item.description: Cyril Imhof: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

item.title: BREAKING: US Government BANNED Mythos and Fable
item.description: Better Stack: A YouTube video claims the US government issued an export control directive forcing Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models for all customers immediately. The claim references an Anthropic news link about the alleged action.

item.title: Google’s New AI Just Broke The AI Speed Limit: DiffusionGemma
item.description: AI Revolution: Google unveiled DiffusionGemma, an open AI model that generates text using a different approach and achieves up to four times faster inference on dedicated GPUs. The model features a 256-token canvas and includes self-correction capabilities.

item.title: Which Antigravity surface should I use? 2.0 vs CLI vs IDE vs SDK
item.description: Google Antigravity: Google Antigravity is an agent-first software development platform that helps developers build, orchestrate, and monitor AI agents. It provides multiple interface options including a standalone desktop app (Antigravity 2.0), IDE integration, CLI, and SDK for flexible agentic workflows.

item.title: AIventure: Vibe Coding Journey
item.description: Google for Developers: AIventure is an open source retro dungeon crawler game built on Angular and Phaser.js that teaches developers about Gen AI through interactive gameplay. It demonstrates vibe coding and agentic behaviors using Gemma 4, with multiple model-serving options including local inference via Transformers.js, Ollama, or LM Studio, as well as cloud routing to Gemini API.

item.title: PI AGENT FULL COURSE: Master Pi Agent in 30 Minutes
item.description: David Ondrej: A course teaching Pi Agent, a framework for building real coding agents. The video promises to teach Pi Agent fundamentals in 30 minutes and covers practical implementation for AI-assisted coding.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 Just Changed Website Design Forever
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts presents a video comparing Claude Fable 5 and Opus 4.8 for website design tasks across three difficulty levels, with emphasis on token efficiency and practical design outcome quality. The video demonstrates Fable 5's capability to extract design DNA and recreate complex website layouts from reference images.

item.title: Claude FM 🎵 music for thinking and building
item.description: Claude: Claude FM is a music service curated by musicians and designed to accompany thinking and building activities. The playlist is made and curated specifically for people working on technical or creative tasks.

item.title: Claude FM 🎵 music for thinking and building
item.description: Claude: Claude FM is a music streaming service made and curated by musicians, designed specifically to support thinking and building work. It is positioned as a productivity tool for developers and creators.

item.title: How to Use Higgsfield AI Like a PRO – AI Marketing Workflow
item.description: AI Master: Higgsfield Supercomputer is an AI super-agent that converts a single idea into full marketing campaigns including TV ads, podcast clips, vertical reels, and character-driven shorts. It automates content creation workflows with consistency in character and style across multiple output formats.

item.title: Converting SQL Server code to PostgreSQL
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates using Gemini to convert SQL Server T-SQL code to PostgreSQL, translating data types and explaining the conversion process. Developers can use Gemini as an AI tutor through Google Cloud's Database Migration Service to learn PostgreSQL while migrating applications to the cloud.

item.title: The Rise of Generative UI for Developers (CopilotKit)
item.description: Better Stack: CopilotKit is a developer framework that enables generative UI by allowing AI agents to render real UI components and share state with React or Next.js applications. It moves beyond bolted-on chatbots to agent-native app architectures with human-in-the-loop approval workflows.

item.title: How I make my AI Agent remember everything
item.description: JavaScript Mastery: JavaScript Mastery demonstrates a technique for implementing persistent memory in AI agents. The approach enables agents to retain and recall information across multiple interactions.

item.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
item.description: EO: Pigment is an AI performance management platform that integrates finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data for enterprise decision-making. Co-founded by Eléonore Crespo, the company has raised over 400 million dollars and counts Figma, Brex, and Anthropic as customers, growing 2x year over year.

item.title: The $20,000 API Key Mistake
item.description: Keith AI: Keith AI presents a case study of a $20,000 incident involving improper API key handling. The video serves as a warning about the financial risks of security mismanagement in AI API usage.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 & Apple’s NVIDIA deal
item.description: IBM Technology: A Mixture of Experts podcast episode hosted by Tim Hwang featuring analysis of Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and its tier routing system, Apple's announced shift to cloud-based AI via NVIDIA partnership at WWDC 2026, and debate on whether AI models can detect sarcasm through multimodal inputs versus context alone.

item.title: The “Final Boss” of AI Workflows: UltraCode
item.description: Keith AI: Keith AI discusses UltraCode as a solution for AI workflows, framing it as an advanced or comprehensive approach to workflow management. The video title suggests UltraCode represents a significant capability in the AI workflow space.

item.title: Master Claude Code with Claude Fable 5 (57 mins)
item.description: Keith AI: Keith AI's 57-minute video demonstrates advanced use of Claude Code as a coding system rather than a chat interface. The video covers Desktop, CLI, and web modes, permissions, connectors, slash commands, plugins, routines, sub-agents, and dynamic workflows for building apps and automating development workflows.

item.title: Code with Claude Tokyo 2026: Opening Keynote
item.description: Claude: Anthropic held an opening keynote at the Code with Claude 2026 conference in Tokyo, featuring engineering and product leaders sharing updates. The event appears to be a major venue for communicating company direction and recent work.

item.title: The Fable 5 Backlash Is Getting Serious
item.description: AI Revolution: Claude Fable 5 faces backlash for refusing harmless prompts, triggering false safety alarms, and limiting AI-related work through opaque guardrails. Users question whether they receive the actual model or a weakened fallback version, raising concerns about trust in closed AI systems.

item.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
item.description: Cyril Imhof: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

item.title: Yossi Matias on the golden age of research
item.description: Google for Developers: Yossi Matias, VP and Head of Google Research, discusses the "magic cycle" of research from breakthrough to real-world impact. The conversation covers AI applications including flood forecasting, MedGemma, Gemini for Science, and tools like Co-Scientist and Empirical Research Assistance, alongside recent advances in speculative decoding, generative UI, and quantum computing.

item.title: Your first agent configuration with MCP and Looker
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates how to build AI-powered agents using the Agent Development Kit (ADK) with Looker integration, focusing on the Model Context Protocol (MCP) for grounding agents in enterprise data. The video covers MCP Toolbox for Databases, tools.yaml configuration, API credential setup, and building a functional agent that translates prompts into Looker data requests.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 + YouTube = $30,000/mo
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts demonstrates a YouTube channel creation system using Claude Fable 5 for automated video editing, combined with script writing, voice recording, and the Higsfield CLI. The approach reverse-engineers successful channels, produces videos for approximately $2 each, and claims to generate $30,000 monthly revenue from faceless or talking-head content.

item.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
item.description: AI Engineer: WorkOS built Studio, an internal workspace where non-technical users can ask business questions in natural language against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion and receive answers or reusable widgets. The LLM generates declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, making subsequent runs deterministic and cost-effective.

item.title: Running 128 Coding Agents at Once
item.description: Cursor: Cursor and Baseten representatives discuss scaling coding agents to run at scale (128 agents in parallel), agent-to-agent communication and review mechanisms, and systems-level approaches to agent orchestration beyond simple parallelization.

item.title: What Should An AI Device Look Like? — With Alex Himel
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: Alex Himel, Meta's VP of Wearables, discusses how AI glasses could become the next major computing platform. The conversation covers AI assistants for daily tasks, competition from OpenAI, Google, Apple and Amazon, privacy concerns around facial recognition, and the role of on-device AI in wearable devices.

item.title: Local AI Agents Masterclass (Gemma 4 + OpenCode + Odysseus tutorial)
item.description: Jordan Urbs: Jordan Urbs demonstrates running two types of local AI agents: Odysseus (workspace agent) and OpenCode (build agent), both executed on personal hardware. The tutorial covers installing Odysseus via Docker, running Gemma 4 locally through Ollama at 7.4GB RAM, and using Claude Opus as a fallback teacher model for self-healing agent skills.

item.title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
item.description: AI Engineer: Tara Agyemang presents WebMCP, a proposed web standard that enables AI agents to interact with web applications through structured tools instead of screen scraping. The standard offers both declarative HTML attributes for simple forms and imperative JavaScript APIs for complex workflows, with early preview in Chrome 146.

item.title: What is MCP? Learn MCP Client, MCP Server & MCP Architecture
item.description: Code With Aarohi: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standard protocol that enables AI applications and agents to connect with external tools, APIs, databases, and services. The protocol defines client-server architecture using JSON-RPC communication over STDIO or HTTP transports, allowing AI agents to access standardized integrations regardless of the underlying agent framework.

item.title: How to Keep Shipping When You Walk Away from Your Desk — Zack Proser, WorkOS
item.description: AI Engineer: Zack Proser presents a four-layer architecture for sustainable AI-assisted development: signal agents monitoring Slack and Linear, verification gates from linting to critic passes, weekly agent analysis of conversation history to surface inefficiencies, and integration of health metrics via Oura ring. The approach treats human attention as the bottleneck and uses voice-first workflows and remote control to enable developers to ship code while away from their desk.

item.title: "An endless demand for compute" | Jonathan Ross, founder of Groq
item.description: Julia Turc: Groq is a hardware chip specifically designed for LLM inference. Jonathan Ross, the founder, discusses Groq's LPU architecture, its comparison to GPUs via Pareto curves and cost-per-token metrics, and a strategic agreement with NVIDIA. The interview covers technical aspects like static scheduling and mixture-of-experts models.

item.title: AI That’s Too Dangerous For You? What we learned from S.A.T.A.N
item.description: IBM Technology: IBM Technology discusses how AI is accelerating the detection of zero-day vulnerabilities and reshaping cybersecurity defenses. The video explains AI-driven vulnerability scanning and DevSecOps as methods to enhance system safety, positioning AI as a tool for strengthening security rather than purely as a risk.

item.title: The AI Skill I use to prevent refactors
item.description: JavaScript Mastery: JavaScript Mastery presents an AI-assisted technique for code maintenance and refactoring prevention. The video discusses a specific AI skill that developers can apply to reduce the need for code refactors.

item.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
item.description: Caleb Writes Code: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 in three sizes: Nano, Super, and Ultra. The models combine hybrid Mamba transformer, Mixture of Experts, and MTP architecture for improved performance in language modeling tasks.

item.title: Opendoor’s India exit is fueling a bigger conversation about AI and outsourcing
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Opendoor's exit from India is prompting broader discussion about the relationship between AI capabilities and outsourcing practices. India's emergence as the world's largest Global Capability Center market frames this decision within larger workforce and economic trends.

item.title: Anthropic’s Dario Amodei has just one direct report
item.description: TechCrunch AI: TechCrunch AI reports that Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has only one direct report, presented as evidence of his leadership approach or effectiveness.

item.title: Microsoft C.E.O. Satya Nadella Says ‘Everyone Is a Stakeholder’ in A.I. - The New York Times
item.description: Google News AI: Microsoft C.E.O. Satya Nadella articulated a position that "Everyone Is a Stakeholder" in artificial intelligence. This statement reflects a governance perspective on inclusive participation in AI development and deployment.

item.title: Anthropic Walks Back Policy That Could Have ‘Sabotaged’ AI Researchers Using Claude
item.description: Simon Willison: Anthropic reversed a controversial policy that would have secretly limited Claude's effectiveness on frontier LLM development tasks without user notification. The company acknowledged making the wrong tradeoff between transparency and safeguards, and committed to making such safeguards visible going forward.

item.title: Tell HN: Anthropic's Fable model is too expensive
item.description: hyhmrright: A user on Anthropic's $200 subscription plan reports that using the Fable model consumed 45% of their weekly quota in a single day for problem-solving and code review, compared to only 80% total usage per week with Opus 4.8. The post criticizes Fable's pricing efficiency relative to user quotas.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 is here!
item.description: AI Search: AI Search reviews Claude Fable 5, demonstrating capabilities across ray tracing, Earth simulation, 3D reconstruction, music composition, cancer classification, and chemistry education. The video compares Fable 5 performance against GPT 5.5 and covers benchmark results and model specifications.
